mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in retail sales indicates robust consumer demand, which translates into higher corporate revenues and earnings expectations, driving S&P 500 forward returns positively over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: If the 3-month annualized growth rate of RSAFS is positive, but S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 55% of such instances over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant increase in the unemployment rate signals a deteriorating labor market, leading to reduced consumer spending and corporate profits, which negatively impacts S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon as recession fears mount.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: If UNRATE rises by more than 0.3pp over 3 months, but S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 40% of such instances over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Funds Rate is high and housing starts are declining, it indicates tight monetary conditions severely impacting interest-rate sensitive sectors, leading to broad market weakness and negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: If FEDFUNDS > 5.0% and HOUST 3-month average change < -10%, but S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of robust economic expansion, strong GDP growth reinforces investor confidence, leading to quick positive reactions in S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon as new data confirms the positive trend.
failure_condition: If GDPC1 annualized growth exceeds 3.0% during an expansionary regime, but S&P 500 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 45% of such periods.

mechanism_narrative: A significant contraction in industrial production signals weakening manufacturing and supply-side activity, indicating a broader economic slowdown that negatively impacts corporate earnings and S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: If INDPRO 3-month average change is less than -0.5%, but S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Persistently high inflation erodes purchasing power and corporate profit margins, leading to expectations of tighter monetary policy and a negative impact on S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon as investors price in higher discount rates.
failure_condition: If CPIAUCSL 12-month change remains above 3.0%, but S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of such instances over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When employment growth is strong but retail sales growth is decelerating, it suggests a divergence where labor market strength is not translating into sustained consumer demand, indicating potential future weakness for S&P 500 over 20 days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month average change > 0.1% AND RSAFS 3-month average change < 0.0%
failure_condition: If PAYEMS shows positive growth while RSAFS shows negative growth, but S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: During a period of monetary tightening, a sharp decline in housing starts signals that higher interest rates are effectively curbing demand, leading to immediate negative sentiment and S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: If HOUST declines by more than 15% in a single month during a tightening cycle, but S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 40% of such instances.
